title: Prove deterministic fixed-point self-host regeneration
goal: Add and pass a fixed-point check showing that self-host generated compiler C remains stable across repeated generations.
context: The current docs mention stable stage-7 C for a small probe. This task should make the fixed-point proof explicit and automated for the complete supported self-host toolchain.
Use temporary directories and deterministic diffs; do not commit generated artifacts unless the repo already expects them.
steps: Create or extend a script that builds stage N and stage N+1 generated C for selfhost tools.
Normalize irrelevant paths or timestamps if needed.
Diff normalized generated C and fail on semantic drift.
Add a Go test or shell gate that runs the fixed-point check.
done_when: A deterministic fixed-point check is part of the automated self-host verification.
The check covers lexer, parser, checker, and codegen selfhost sources.
Docs state exactly what fixed point is proven.
